title: Comanthera vernonioides
taxonomy: Comanthera vernonioides is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a part of the monocot order Poales. Further classification places it in the subclass Commelinidae and the class Liliopsida. The order Poales is within the larger clade commelinids. This classification helps to identify Comanthera vernonioides as a distinct species within the monocot group.

intro: Comanthera vernonioides, also known as Vernonia-like Comanthera, is a type of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Comanthera vernonioides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments.
